prompt: Build a Offline Learning Resource Exchange & Supplier Fulfillment Console that lets district staff and school users manage locally hosted learning resources, track supplier delivery performance, and run compliance-grade moderation without any internet dependency. The user experience is delivered as server-rendered pages using Templ, with fast, form-driven flows and immediate inline feedback for validation and status changes. Roles include Regular User (teachers and staff who discover and engage with resources), Author (uploads and maintains entries), Reviewer/Moderator (handles review queues and reports), Supplier User (confirms fulfillment milestones and quality results), and Administrator (configures rules, permissions, and analytics access). Users can search the catalog with keyword search plus pinyin and synonym typo correction, then filter and sort by author, category, and tags; the home view also shows personalized recommendations based on local activity history and offers "bestseller" and "new release" rankings that reset weekly every Monday at 2:00 AM. Engagement features include upvote/downvote, favorites, and following authors/topics, plus a visible points/levels/badges system where configurable rules award, for example, 5 points for a new approved entry, 1 point for a received upvote, and a 10-point penalty for a moderated takedown; levels advance every 200 points and badges are granted on thresholds such as 50 favorites received. The messaging center provides configurable in-app notifications for entry deadlines, review decisions, publish completion, supplier shipment notices, and anomaly alerts; it supports subscriptions by event type, read/unread state, and an offline retry queue that reattempts delivery to the user inbox up to 5 times over 30 minutes if a write fails. The backend uses Gin to expose REST-style APIs consumed by the Templ-rendered UI and to process supplier portal integrations for order intake, delivery-date confirmation within 48 hours of order creation, advance shipment notice/ASN submission, and QC result feedback within 24 hours of receipt. PostgreSQL persists all entities and maintains KPI rollups per supplier with tiering rules recalculated nightly at 1:00 AM: on-time delivery (target ≥ 95%), stockout rate (target ≤ 2% of order lines), return rate (target ≤ 1% within 30 days), and defect rate (target ≤ 0.5% of inspected units); tiers are Bronze/Silver/Gold and drive dashboard visibility and escalation thresholds. Moderation and risk control includes automated gates (rate limits such as 20 posts per hour per user, and anomaly checks that flag like-ring patterns such as more than 15 mutual likes between the same two accounts in 24 hours) plus manual review workflows with evidence chains, report handling, user bans (1 day/7 days/permanent), and immutable audit logs. Audit and compliance traceability records key actions (import/edit/review/publish/export) with operator, timestamp, before/after values, source, and reason, supports queries by user and time range, and generates export files saved to local disk only. Security is offline-first: authentication is local username/password with minimum 12 characters and lockout after 5 failed attempts for 15 minutes; sensitive fields (password hashes, supplier contact details) are encrypted at rest and masked on-screen for non-admins, and all administrative actions write append-only audit entries with a traceable change history. Analytics dashboards are permission-isolated and run from local aggregates, reporting utilization peaks, equipment demand hotspots, approval cycle time, violation rate, and cancellation rate, with scheduled report generation to local files and no external integrations.
